description:
You are a QA engineer who must take a one-off backup of an existing test environment and record exactly what was archived.

Initial system state
--------------------
1. Directory with test files:
   /home/user/projects/test_env/

   ├── config.yaml
   ├── test_data.csv
   └── readme.md

2. A destination directory for backups does NOT yet exist.

Goal
----
A. Create a compressed archive named
      /home/user/backups/test_env_backup.tar.gz
   that contains the entire test_env directory (the directory itself must be inside the archive, not just its contents).

B. Produce a plain-text log file
      /home/user/backups/backup_log.txt
   whose exact content and layout follow the template below.

Log-file template (must match **exactly**, including capitalisation, colons and indentation):
-------------------------------------------------------
DATE: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS UTC
ARCHIVE: /home/user/backups/test_env_backup.tar.gz
FILES:
  - test_env/config.yaml
  - test_env/test_data.csv
  - test_env/readme.md
SHA256: <64-character hexadecimal checksum of /home/user/backups/test_env_backup.tar.gz>
-------------------------------------------------------

• “DATE” must be the current UTC time, formatted exactly as shown (24-hour clock, leading zeros, a single space before “UTC”).
• “FILES” must list the three files exactly in the order shown, each preceded by two spaces, a hyphen and a space.
• “SHA256” must be the correct SHA-256 hash of the archive you just created.

Summary of what the automated tests will check
----------------------------------------------
1. /home/user/backups/ exists and is a directory.
2. /home/user/backups/test_env_backup.tar.gz exists and, when listed with “tar -tzf”, contains:
     test_env/
     test_env/config.yaml
     test_env/test_data.csv
     test_env/readme.md
   (directory entries may or may not have trailing “/”; both are accepted).
3. /home/user/backups/backup_log.txt exists and:
   • Its first line starts with “DATE:” and matches the required format.
   • The “ARCHIVE:” line points to the correct absolute path.
   • The “FILES:” section lists the three paths exactly as shown.
   • The “SHA256:” line contains a 64-char hexadecimal string that is the real SHA-256 checksum of the archive file.
4. No extraneous files are created inside /home/user/backups/.

Your task: perform whatever shell commands are necessary (no more than 3–4 commands are needed) so that all the above conditions are satisfied.
truth:
Pre-created files and their exact contents:

1. /home/user/projects/test_env/config.yaml
------------------------------------------------
env: staging
version: 1.0
------------------------------------------------

2. /home/user/projects/test_env/test_data.csv
------------------------------------------------
id,value
1,foo
2,bar
------------------------------------------------

3. /home/user/projects/test_env/readme.md
------------------------------------------------
# Test Environment
This directory contains configuration and data for QA tests.
------------------------------------------------

Automated verification details:

• The grader will calculate EXPECTED_SHA="$(sha256sum /home/user/backups/test_env_backup.tar.gz | awk '{print $1}')" and will check that the last line of backup_log.txt is exactly "SHA256: ${EXPECTED_SHA}".

• DATE line is validated against the regex:
  ^DATE: [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2} UTC$

• FILES block must match the three lines (with exact spacing):
  "  - test_env/config.yaml"
  "  - test_env/test_data.csv"
  "  - test_env/readme.md"

No root privileges are required; everything is under /home/user.
